findings.title: Four-step video processing pipeline (HQ-VSR / Table 1d) absent from repo
findings.quote:
- [ ] Release the video processing pipeline.
- [x] Release HQ-VSR dataset.
findings.claim: The paper's Sec. 3.3 / Fig. 3 video processing pipeline (metadata, scene, quality filtering with CLIP-IQA/FasterVQA/DOVER thresholds, and optical-flow motion-area cropping) that constructs HQ-VSR and produces the Table 1d ablation (+Filter / +Motion) is not implemented anywhere in the repo; `finetune/scripts/prepare_dataset.py` only enumerates file paths, and the README explicitly lists the pipeline as an unreleased TODO.
findings.concern: Table 1d (ablation on processing pipeline) and the HQ-VSR construction cannot be reproduced from the code; the dataset is shared but the curation procedure that the paper credits for the performance gains is not.
findings.resolution: Authors: release the metadata/scene/quality-filtering and motion-area-cropping scripts (Eq. 8) so the +Filter and +Motion rows of Table 1d are reproducible.
findings.reason: README lines 80-81 exactly match the quoted TODO; prepare_dataset.py contains no filtering/quality/motion logic, confirming the pipeline is absent.

findings.title: DOVER and FasterVQA eval scripts import packages absent from repo and requirements
findings.quote: from DOVER.evaluate_a_set_of_videos import evaluate_set as dover
findings.claim: `eval_dover.py` imports a `DOVER` package and `eval_vbench.py:149` imports `from evaluate import calculate_final` after `chdir` into a `VBench/` directory; neither `DOVER` nor `VBench` exists in the repo (confirmed by `_audit_code/check_eval_imports.py`), and neither is listed in `requirements.txt`.
findings.concern: DOVER and FasterVQA columns appear for every dataset in Table 2 and in Fig. 1 / Table 3, but the scripts that compute them cannot run without external, unpinned third-party checkouts the README itself flags as TODO.
findings.resolution: Authors: vendor or pin the exact DOVER and VBench/FasterVQA versions (commit hashes) and add the import/setup instructions, or provide a self-contained metric script.
findings.paper_ref: Table 2 (DOVER, FasterVQA columns); README TODO line
findings.reason: eval_dover.py line 150 exactly matches the quoted import; DOVER and VBench are absent from both the repo and requirements.txt.

findings.title: Core eval/inference deps (pyiqa, diffusers, safetensors) not in requirements.txt
findings.claim: `requirements.txt` omits `pyiqa` (used by `eval_metrics.py` and `inference_script.py` for every IQA metric), `diffusers` (the CogVideoX pipeline backbone), and `safetensors`; they are only mentioned as loose, unpinned `pip install` lines in the README (`pip install diffusers["torch"] ...`, `pip install pyiqa`).
findings.concern: The environment cannot be rebuilt from `requirements.txt` alone, and the diffusers version is unpinned even though the pipeline depends on specific CogVideoX scheduler/transformer APIs (e.g. `scheduler.get_velocity`).
findings.resolution: Authors: add and pin `pyiqa`, `diffusers`, and `safetensors` (with versions) in `requirements.txt`.
findings.reason: requirements.txt (20 lines) contains none of pyiqa, diffusers, or safetensors; README lines 98-99 confirm these are loose unpinned pip install instructions only.

findings.title: inference.sh scores SPMCS/YouHQ40/RealVSR/MVSR4x against UDM10 ground truth
findings.claim: Every `eval_metrics.py` invocation in `inference.sh` hardcodes `--gt datasets/test/UDM10/GT`, including the SPMCS, YouHQ40, RealVSR, and MVSR4x blocks (lines 22-25, 34-37, 47-50, 60-63); only the UDM10 block uses its own GT. `_audit_code/check_inference_gt_paths.py` confirms 5 of 6 calls are dataset-mismatched.
findings.concern: Full-reference metrics (PSNR/SSIM/LPIPS/DISTS) in Table 2 for these four datasets are computed against the wrong dataset's ground truth; `eval_metrics.py` matches files by name stem (`process` line 174), so non-matching clips are silently skipped (yielding count=0 / no output) and any accidental name collisions produce meaningless scores — either way the provided script does not reproduce Table 2's FR columns for these datasets.
findings.resolution: Change each block's `--gt` to the matching `datasets/test/<DatasetName>/GT`. The script's own intent (per-dataset evaluation) is contradicted by the copy-paste UDM10 path.
findings.paper_ref: Table 2 (PSNR/SSIM/LPIPS/DISTS rows for SPMCS, YouHQ40, RealVSR, MVSR4x)
findings.reason: inference.sh lines 22-25, 34-37, 47-50, and 60-63 all confirmed to hardcode --gt datasets/test/UDM10/GT for SPMCS, YouHQ40, RealVSR, and MVSR4x blocks respectively.

findings.title: eval_ewarp.py imports a non-existent `ewarp` module and a wrong RAFT/model path
findings.quote:
original_dir = os.getcwd()
raft_dir = os.path.join(original_dir, "RAFT")
os.chdir(raft_dir)
from ewarp import Ewarp as Ewarp
results, avg_score = Ewarp(args)
findings.claim: `eval_ewarp.py` (the only script for the E*warp temporal-consistency metric) chdirs into `<script_dir>/RAFT` and does `from ewarp import Ewarp`, but there is no `ewarp.py` anywhere in the repo and no `finetune/scripts/RAFT/` directory (RAFT lives at `finetune/utils/RAFT/`); the default `--model finetune/scripts/models/raft-things.pth` (line 190) also does not exist (weights are at `finetune/utils/RAFT/raft-things.pth`). All three confirmed by `_audit_code/check_eval_imports.py`.
findings.concern: The E*warp column in Table 2 and Fig. 1 cannot be computed: the script raises FileNotFoundError on the chdir / ImportError on `from ewarp import Ewarp` before any metric is produced; this matches the README TODO that E*warp computation is unreleased.
findings.resolution: Authors: add the missing `ewarp.py` (the RAFT-based warping-error implementation) and fix the RAFT directory and default `--model` paths to point at `finetune/utils/RAFT/`.
findings.reason: eval_ewarp.py lines 146-150 exactly match the quote; no RAFT directory under finetune/scripts/, RAFT exists under finetune/utils/, and ewarp.py is absent from the entire repo.

findings.title: eval_dover.py maps per-sample DOVER scores with an off-by-one (i-1) shift
findings.quote:
for i, name in enumerate(pred_names):
    results[name] = dover_results[i-1]
findings.claim: When assembling per-sample DOVER scores, the first sorted clip (`i=0`) is assigned `dover_results[-1]` (the last clip's score) and every other clip is shifted by one. `_audit_code/check_dover_offbyone.py` reproduces this: all N per-sample entries are misaligned, while the reported average is unchanged because it sums the same multiset.
findings.concern: Per-sample DOVER values written to `metrics_dover.json` are wrong (clip ↔ score mismatch); the paper reports only dataset averages, so headline Table 2 DOVER numbers are not affected, but any per-clip analysis or future use of this output would be incorrect.
findings.reason: eval_dover.py line 159 confirmed as `results[name] = dover_results[i-1]` with enumerate starting at i=0, producing the described off-by-one shift.

findings.title: Paper says DIV2K '900 images'; code/README use standard DIV2K_train_HR (800)
findings.quote: The image dataset is DIV2K [3], with 900 images
findings.claim: Sec. 4.1 states the image training set is DIV2K with 900 images, but `train_ddp_one_s2.sh:30` loads `DIV2K_train_HR.txt` and the README datasets table (line 120) lists DIV2K-HR as 800 images — the standard DIV2K training-HR split size.
findings.concern: The stated image count (900) does not match the standard DIV2K_train_HR split (800) referenced by the code and README; minor, but the exact training-image set affects the Stage-2 pixel-space ablations.
findings.resolution: Authors: confirm whether 900 vs 800 reflects extra images (e.g. validation HR) or is a typo; specify the exact image list used.
findings.reason: paper_text.txt confirms the paper states 'DIV2K [3], with 900 images'; README line 120 lists DIV2K-HR as 800 images, confirming the 900 vs 800 discrepancy.
